claim_boundary: Ticket 46 refutes the tested finite scalar clause-rank proof routes and isolates the next ordinal or stateful-measure obligation. It does not prove or disprove RH, Collatz, Goldbach, or Twin Prime.

attempts.attempt: Transfer TICKET46's rule: a finite symbolic grammar that only works by adding a horizon/depth escape coordinate cannot be promoted to a proof. It must either become a compact invariant theorem or produce a genuine counterexample.

bounded_result.discarded_shortcut: finite zero grammar repaired by a height-dependent escape coordinate
bounded_result.retained_target: A compact zero-clause grammar or positive kernel whose margin is invariant under all height extensions, not reselected from the checked height.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: an off-critical zero or kernel-pressure cycle that survives every compact repair

attempts.obstruction: The project has improved the falsification protocol for proposed proof grammars, but this transfer is not an infinite proof and not a certified counterexample.
attempts.candidate_theorem: A compact zero-clause grammar or positive kernel whose margin is invariant under all height extensions, not reselected from the checked height.
attempts.next_experiment: Audit whether zero-feature repairs depend on height; extract the first off-critical pressure cycle.
attempts.claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Stress-test every TICKET45 symbolic clause grammar at the first horizon where the modulo-16 phase wrap is visible. A grammar is discarded if its 28-bit nonnegative-pressure quotient is cyclic. Candidate repairs that require depth or unwrapped phase are audited as escape coordinates rather than accepted as proofs.

wrap_stress_trials_28.description: One rank clause per phase modulo 16.
wrap_stress_trials_28.proof_intent: Test whether phase alone can define a horizon-independent rank.

scale_interval.proof_boundary: A nonnegative-pressure cycle in the clause graph blocks any scalar rank on this clause family.

wrap_stress_trials_28.interpretation: This refutes or supports only the named symbolic clause family on the bounded template graph. It is not a proof or disproof of Collatz.

wrap_stress_trials_28.description: Phase plus coarse valuation-tail mass and clipped next-valuation.
wrap_stress_trials_28.proof_intent: Test a small symbolic rank family that ignores residue identity.

scale_interval.proof_boundary: A nonnegative-pressure cycle in the clause graph blocks any scalar rank on this clause family.

wrap_stress_trials_28.description: The observed 26-bit template identity used by TICKET43.
wrap_stress_trials_28.proof_intent: Use the observed template table as a ceiling control: it may certify the bounded sample, but it is not a horizon-independent theorem.

scale_interval.proof_boundary: A nonnegative-pressure cycle in the clause graph blocks any scalar rank on this clause family.

wrap_stress_trials_28.interpretation: This refutes or supports only the named symbolic clause family on the bounded template graph. It is not a proof or disproof of Collatz.

escape_coordinate_audit.why_it_is_not_a_proof_yet: The current template key stores phase modulo 16. An unwrapped epoch must be supplied by the external lift depth or path history, so it is not a finite horizon-independent clause grammar.
escape_coordinate_audit.required_next_theorem: Define a well-founded ordinal or monotone epoch generated by the Collatz transition itself, then prove every future lift decreases it without rechoosing the epoch from max_bits.

escape_coordinate_audit.why_it_is_not_a_proof_yet: A depth bucket can break the observed wrap cycle only because it remembers which bounded horizon created the edge. A proof needs one fixed object that does not grow when 29, 30, or later bits are added.
escape_coordinate_audit.required_next_theorem: Replace finite depth buckets by a compact transition rule with an invariant descent, or extract a future edge that refutes that compact rule.

escape_coordinate_audit.why_it_is_not_a_proof_yet: The exact template table was a bounded ceiling certificate at 26 bits. At 28 bits it has 52547 new clauses and 187086 new nonnegative-pressure edges, and its 28-bit pressure graph is cyclic.
escape_coordinate_audit.required_next_theorem: Either compress the exact table into a stable symbolic grammar with acyclic pressure graph, or prove an ordinal-valued measure that tolerates the pressure cycles.

stable_clause_grammar_audit.restricted_no_go_statement: Within the five tested finite template-local scalar clause-rank grammars, no candidate remains after 28-bit wrap stress: every tested quotient has a nonnegative-pressure cycle.
stable_clause_grammar_audit.closed_bounded_statement: The 28-bit lift refutes phase-only, coarse tail mass, residue-16, residue-64, and exact observed template scalar clause-rank proof routes by pressure-cycle extraction.
stable_clause_grammar_audit.proof_boundary: No Collatz proof and no Collatz counterexample. TICKET46 proves only a restricted no-go theorem for the tested scalar, template-local clause-rank routes. Surviving approaches must introduce a rigorously defined ordinal/stateful measure or find a true Collatz counterexample.

attempts.obstruction: The phase-wrap edge does not merely defeat a coarse quotient. At 28 bits every tested template-local scalar clause grammar becomes pressure-cyclic, including the exact observed template table. The obvious repair is to add an epoch/depth coordinate, but that coordinate is horizon-dependent unless a new well-founded transition theorem is proved.
attempts.candidate_theorem: There exists a compact, horizon-independent ordinal or stateful measure on Collatz lift templates that strictly decreases on every future lifted open edge, including phase-wrap edges, without rechoosing the measure at each max_bits.
attempts.next_experiment: Generate TICKET47 by synthesizing ordinal/stateful measures whose state update is template-local and then extracting a 29-bit or 30-bit counteredge whenever the update secretly depends on horizon depth.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof and no certified Collatz counterexample.
